sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Noah, presents with a 1-week history of back pain, which started after a twisting injury to the left side.
He reports the pain is located in the middle of the back, lower side, and sometimes radiates to the left side, particularly with sudden movements to the left.
The pain is exacerbated by twisting and is located in the center of the back, extending to the lower back and left side.
The patient denies any other symptoms.
He has a history of diabetes and has been taking metformin twice a day.
He reports his blood sugar levels have been higher than usual, but he tries to exercise and control his diet.
He has a family history of owning a bakery, and he admits to frequenting the bakery and consuming sweet treats.
He denies any smoking, alcohol, or drug use.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure, pulse rate, oxygen saturation, and temperature were not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's lungs were clear, and his heart had a regular rate and rhythm.
The doctor performed a physical exam, which included pressing down on the patient's back, starting at the top and working down.
The patient reported pain in the center of the back, lower side, and left side, but not on the right side.
The doctor also examined the patient's lower extremities and found no pain, numbness, or tingling in the feet.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I believe the patient has a musculoskeletal low back strain.
Problem list: Low back strain; Diabetes mellitus; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed 400mg of ibuprofen as needed for pain relief, up to 3 times a day, and instructed the patient to take it with Tylenol.
I also prescribed 1 tablet of Flexeril daily for muscle relaxation, cautioning against strenuous activities after taking it.
I ordered an x-ray to evaluate the joint spaces and potential disk issues.
I also ordered physical therapy to help with pain relief and muscle strengthening.
I instructed the patient to continue taking metformin and to continue controlling his diet and exercise routine.
I ordered an A1c test to evaluate his diabetes control.
Follow-up:
I instructed the patient to return for a follow-up appointment to discuss the results of the x-ray and A1c test.
